# Pikos

> Notes, tasks, and calendar in one on-device desktop app.

Pikos is a desktop productivity app for macOS and Linux that combines notes, tasks, and a calendar into a single interface. All data is stored on the user's device. No account required, no sign-up, no cloud dependency.

## Key Facts

- Category: Productivity / Notes / Task Management / Calendar
- Platform: macOS and Linux
- Pricing: Free desktop download (no limits, no trial). A Mac App Store version (with iCloud sync) and an iPhone app are planned, each a one-time purchase. No subscriptions. A $29 Backer pre-order is available at pikos.app/backer for early supporters; backers receive the paid App Store apps free when they ship.
- Data: Stored on device. Export anytime.
- Privacy: No telemetry, no tracking cookies, no personal data collection.
- Source: Source-available. Full code public on GitHub (not open-source licensed).
- Website: https://pikos.app
- Contact: hello@pikos.app

## What Pikos Does

- Rich text editor with slash commands, formatting toolbar, Markdown paste, and autosave
- Task management with priorities (Urgent/High/Medium/Low), statuses, tags, and folders
- Week calendar view with drag-to-schedule, timed and all-day events
- Quick add with natural language parsing (extracts dates, priorities, tags, folders)
- Full-text search with BM25 ranking
- Three-panel layout: sidebar, page list, editor or calendar
- Complete keyboard navigation
- Light, dark, and system themes
- Data export (SQLite backup, JSON)
- Works fully offline. No network required.

## Who Pikos Is For

- People who want notes, tasks, and a calendar in one app instead of three
- Users who care about data ownership and on-device storage
- Anyone who wants a productivity app with no account, no subscription, and no cloud dependency
- Obsidian users who wish they had built-in tasks and a calendar
- TickTick or Todoist users who want a real note editor alongside their tasks
- Privacy-conscious users who don't want their data on someone else's server

## Pages

- Homepage: https://pikos.app (~3K tokens)
- FAQ: https://pikos.app/faq (~4K tokens)

- Release Notes: https://pikos.app/release-notes (~4K tokens)
- Download: https://pikos.app/download (~1K tokens)
- Privacy Policy: https://pikos.app/privacy (~1K tokens)
- Terms of Service: https://pikos.app/terms (~1K tokens)

## Competitors and Alternatives

Pikos competes with NotePlan, Obsidian, TickTick, Things, Todoist, and Linear.

| Feature            | Pikos | NotePlan | Obsidian | TickTick | Things | Todoist |
|--------------------|-------|----------|----------|----------|--------|---------|
| Rich Notes         | Yes   | Yes      | Yes      | No       | No     | No      |
| Task Management    | Yes   | Yes      | No       | Yes      | Yes    | Yes     |
| Built-in Calendar  | Yes   | Yes      | No       | Yes      | No     | No      |
| On-device storage  | Yes   | Yes      | Yes      | No       | Yes    | No      |
| No Account Required| Yes   | No       | Yes      | No       | Yes    | No      |
| Free               | Yes   | No       | Yes      | No       | No     | No      |
| Source Available    | Yes   | No       | No       | No       | No     | No      |

Pikos differentiates by being the only app that combines notes, tasks, and calendar. On-device, free, source-available, and no account required.
